issues.message: Sequential async I/O in readNoTicketsDir: stat() and readFile() are awaited one at a time in a for loop, serialising all filesystem reads
issues.suggestion: Collect all top-level items then fan out with Promise.all — one Promise.all for stat calls, then another for readFile calls on matched items. The same pattern applies to subdirectory reads.

issues.message: readNoTicketsDir is a verbatim copy of the same sequential-I/O function from src/cli.ts, duplicating both the bottleneck and the maintenance surface
issues.suggestion: Extract readNoTicketsDir into a shared utility (e.g. src/core/fs.ts) and parallelise the I/O there with Promise.all. Both callers benefit from a single fix.

issues.message: JSON.stringify used for deep equality of the optional meta field on every feature comparison. Serialising arbitrary objects on every diffFeature call is unnecessarily expensive when meta is absent or shallow.
issues.suggestion: Guard with a fast-path: if both values are undefined or strictly equal, skip the stringify. For non-trivial meta objects a recursive structural equality helper avoids serialisation overhead entirely.

issues.message: Array.filter() allocates a transient array solely to obtain its length when counting completed tasks during feature state construction
issues.suggestion: Replace with a reduce or a simple for-loop counter: feature.tasks.reduce((n, t) => t.status === completed ? n + 1 : n, 0). This avoids the intermediate array allocation.

issues.message: readStdin wraps each incoming chunk in Buffer.from() before pushing it, creating an unnecessary copy of every chunk. The original chunk is already a Buffer-compatible Uint8Array.
issues.suggestion: Push the raw chunk directly: chunks.push(chunk as Buffer). Buffer.concat accepts Uint8Array items, so the intermediate copy is not needed.
